content_en:
The relationship between Al-Isabah and the hadith sciences is inseparable. Ibn Hajar composed the work as a hadith scholar, and the work serves hadith scholarship in specific and important ways. Understanding this relationship helps explain why the work was so immediately accepted as authoritative and why it remains in constant use.

In hadith criticism, a crucial stage in evaluating any transmitted report is examining the chain of transmission (isnad). If a chain claims to include a companion, the hadith critic must be able to verify that the named companion actually existed and that the claimed connection between that companion and the subsequent transmitter is plausible. Al-Isabah provides the first half of this verification: it documents which companions existed and can therefore appear legitimately in chains of transmission.

The work also documents what each companion was known to have transmitted. The entries often include the number of hadiths attributed to a companion, their major students among the tabi'un, and any known information about their reliability. This information is directly relevant to evaluating whether specific hadiths attributed to specific companions are plausible.

One of the recurring problems in hadith criticism is the occurrence of chains in which a known companion appears to transmit a hadith to a tabi'i scholar who could not have met that companion given what is known about the companion's location, death date, or movements. Al-Isabah helps hadith critics identify these anachronisms by providing the biographical details that make such inconsistencies detectable.

Ibn Hajar's own use of Al-Isabah in his other hadith works — particularly Fath al-Bari — is extensive. When he discusses hadiths in the Fath, he regularly refers to the companion transmitter's biography and provides cross-references to Al-Isabah. This integration of biographical and hadith-critical analysis is characteristic of his scholarly method and reflects the organic connection between the two fields.

For contemporary scholars and students, Al-Isabah continues to serve these functions. Digital editions and online databases have made its reference function even more accessible, allowing rapid lookup of companion biographies while working through hadith chains.
content_id:
Hubungan antara Al-Isabah dan ilmu hadis tidak dapat dipisahkan. Ibnu Hajar menyusun karya ini sebagai seorang ulama hadis, dan karya ini melayani ilmu hadis dengan cara-cara yang spesifik dan penting. Memahami hubungan ini membantu menjelaskan mengapa karya ini segera diterima sebagai otoritatif dan mengapa ia tetap digunakan secara konstan.

Dalam kritik hadis, tahap yang sangat penting dalam mengevaluasi laporan yang diteruskan adalah memeriksa sanad (isnad). Jika sebuah sanad mengklaim mencakup seorang sahabat, kritikus hadis harus dapat memverifikasi bahwa sahabat yang disebutkan benar-benar ada dan bahwa hubungan yang diklaim antara sahabat tersebut dan perawi berikutnya masuk akal. Al-Isabah menyediakan setengah pertama dari verifikasi ini: ia mendokumentasikan sahabat mana yang ada dan karenanya dapat muncul secara sah dalam sanad-sanad.

Karya ini juga mendokumentasikan apa yang masing-masing sahabat diketahui telah meriwayatkan. Entri-entri sering menyertakan jumlah hadis yang dinisbatkan kepada sahabat, murid-murid utama mereka di antara tabi'in, dan informasi yang diketahui tentang keandalan mereka. Informasi ini langsung relevan untuk mengevaluasi apakah hadis-hadis tertentu yang dinisbatkan kepada sahabat tertentu masuk akal.

Salah satu masalah yang berulang dalam kritik hadis adalah terjadinya sanad di mana sahabat yang dikenal tampaknya meriwayatkan hadis kepada ulama tabi'i yang tidak mungkin bertemu dengan sahabat tersebut mengingat apa yang diketahui tentang lokasi, tanggal kematian, atau pergerakan sahabat tersebut. Al-Isabah membantu para kritikus hadis mengidentifikasi anakronisme-anakronisme ini dengan menyediakan detail-detail biografis yang membuat inkonsistensi tersebut dapat dideteksi.

Penggunaan Ibnu Hajar sendiri atas Al-Isabah dalam karya-karya hadisnya yang lain — khususnya Fath al-Bari — sangat ekstensif. Ketika ia membahas hadis-hadis dalam Fath, ia secara teratur merujuk pada biografi sahabat yang meriwayatkan dan memberikan referensi silang ke Al-Isabah. Untuk para sarjana dan pelajar kontemporer, Al-Isabah terus melayani fungsi-fungsi ini.
content_ar: The relationship between Al-Isabah and the الحديث sciences is inseparable. Ibn Hajar composed the work as a الحديث scholar, and the work serves الحديث scholarship in specific and المهم ways. الفهم this relationship helps explain why the work was لذا immediately accepted as المعتمد and why it remains in constant use. In الحديث criticism, a البالغ الأهمية stage in evaluating any transmitted report is examining the chain of transmission (الإسناد). إذا a chain claims to include a companion, the الحديث critic must be able to verify that the named companion actually existed and that the claimed connection between that companion and the subsequent transmitter is plausible. Al-Isabah provides the أولاً half of this verification: it documents which الصحابة existed and can لذلك appear legitimately in chains of transmission. The work أيضاً documents what each companion was known to have transmitted. The entries often include the number of hadiths attributed to a companion, their major students among the tabi'un, and any known information about their reliability. This information is directly relevant to evaluating whether specific hadiths attributed to specific الصحابة are plausible. One of the recurring problems in الحديث criticism is the occurrence of chains in which a known companion appears to transmit a الحديث to a tabi'i scholar who could not have met that companion given what is known about the companion's location, الموت date, or movements. Al-Isabah helps الحديث critics identify these anachronisms by providing the biographical details that make such inconsistencies detectable. Ibn Hajar's own use of Al-Isabah in his other الحديث works — particularly Fath al-Bari — is extensive. عندما he discusses hadiths in the Fath, he regularly refers to the companion transmitter's biography and provides cross-references to Al-Isabah. This integration of biographical and الحديث-البالغ الأهمية analysis is characteristic of his العلمي method and reflects the organic connection between the two fields. For contemporary العلماء and students, Al-Isabah continues to serve these functions. Digital editions and online databases have made its reference function even more accessible, allowing rapid lookup of companion biographies بينما working through الحديث chains.
